summary: Validated Share URL feature (Studio) + new /compare A vs B page on LindArt. Backend regression PASSED (9/9 tests in test_lindart_api.py: health + full Palette CRUD + favorite filter + 404 paths; AI generation deselected per request to skip slow LLM call — previously validated). Frontend: share-palette-btn copies a correct /studio?c=HEX-HEX-...&n=...&s=... URL with toast 'Link da paleta copiado'; importing /studio?c=0D1B2A-1B263B-415A77-E0E1DD&n=Oceano correctly activates a shared palette named 'Oceano', shows import toast, and cleans the querystring (replaceState). /compare page renders with compare-page, compare-a-select / compare-b-select (12 options), compare-swap-btn swaps A↔B, metrics compare-a-contrast (e.g. '2.02 WCAG') and compare-a-diversity (e.g. '54%') display, contrast-matrix shows base-A vs B and base-B vs A with WCAG contrast ratios. compare-a-share copies the palette's /studio?c=... URL with toast 'Link copiado'. Navbar shows 'Comparar' (nav-link-compare), MobileNav shows 'A/B' (mobile-nav-compare). Regression smoke on /, /studio, /library, /calculator, /tips all load with 0 console errors. ONE CRITICAL MOBILE BUG FOUND: the platform-injected #emergent-badge overlaps the right side of MobileNav at 390px viewport — elementFromPoint at the center of mobile-nav-compare returns the emergent-badge anchor, meaning real users cannot tap 'A/B', 'Custo', or 'Dicas' on mobile.

ui_bugs.issue: #emergent-badge ('Made with Emergent') overlays the right ~50% of the bottom mobile nav at viewport 390px. Items 4-6 (mobile-nav-compare 'A/B', mobile-nav-calculator 'Custo', mobile-nav-tips 'Dicas') are not reachable by user tap — document.elementFromPoint at the center of mobile-nav-compare returns <a id='emergent-badge'>. Even Playwright force=True clicks don't fire React onClick because hit-testing routes to the badge anchor.

issues: 6 items + Emergent badge is too many for a 390px row; consider reducing to 5 items, hiding labels on the rightmost items, adding padding-right to leave space for the badge, or moving the badge above the nav.

action_items: MOBILE NAV FIX (HIGH): The Emergent badge covers right side of MobileNav. Suggested fix in /app/frontend/src/components/MobileNav.jsx — add `pr-32` (or ~120px right padding) to the <ul> on small screens so the 6 icons compress to the left, OR remove one less-used item (e.g., 'Dicas' or 'Custo') from mobile bottom nav and surface it elsewhere, OR set `pointer-events-none` for the badge wrapper in mobile (not recommended since it's platform-owned). Verify on real 390px viewport that all 6 items are tappable and not covered by #emergent-badge.
critical_code_review_comments: Studio.jsx useEffect: decodePaletteFromSearch runs only on mount with empty dep array. If a user navigates inside the app from /studio?c=... via React Router (no full reload), the effect won't fire. Currently fine because the share URL is opened as a full navigation, but document this constraint.

Studio.jsx setSharedPalette sets the shared palette and setActivePalette in the same render. allPalettes useMemo depends on sharedPalette — works correctly, but the shared palette has id `shared-<hexes>` which can collide if two users share the exact same hex sequence with different names (unlikely but worth noting).

share.js padEnd('0',6).slice(0,6) silently accepts malformed inputs (e.g., 3-char hex) producing nonsense colors. Consider validating hex length === 6 and rejecting the import with a toast.error instead of importing a corrupted palette.

Compare.jsx paletteContrastScore averages contrast of base vs others only — semantically OK but the 'VENCE' label compares raw averages which can mislead when one palette has very few colors. Consider noting palette size in the metric or normalizing.

Compare.jsx aId/bId initial state is computed from PRESET_PALETTES on first render; if the user has saved palettes that load asynchronously via loadSaved(), the select options expand but aId/bId stay on initial values (still works — just informational).

MobileNav.jsx has 6 items in a flex justify-between row at 390px — this is already tight even without the Emergent badge. Each item is ~60px wide which is fine, but with the badge the rightmost items are unreachable (see HIGH bug above).

success_rate.backend: 100% (9/9 non-AI tests; AI skipped per request)
success_rate.frontend: 95% — Share URL + /compare fully functional; mobile-nav-compare/calculator/tips visually present but blocked by platform badge overlay

test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created in this iteration. Backend CRUD tests create and delete a TEST_ palette in the same test run (clean).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Share URL flow verified: clicking [data-testid='share-palette-btn'] copies `${origin}/studio?c=HEX-HEX-HEX-HEX&n=<name>&s=<style>` and shows toast 'Link da paleta copiado'. Opening that URL imports the palette as active with id `shared-<hexes>` and clears querystring via history.replaceState. /compare page selectors validated: compare-page, compare-a-select, compare-b-select, compare-swap-btn, compare-a-contrast, compare-a-diversity, compare-b-contrast, compare-b-diversity, contrast-matrix, matrix-a-vs-b, matrix-b-vs-a, compare-a-share, compare-b-share. Desktop nav-link-compare label='Comparar'; mobile-nav-compare label='A/B'. The mobile bug is platform-level overlay (#emergent-badge), fixable by adjusting MobileNav <ul> padding-right or reducing mobile item count. After fix, retest mobile-nav-compare / calculator / tips clickability at 390px viewport.
rca of the issue: RCA — Mobile nav unreachable items: The Emergent preview platform injects a fixed-position anchor (id='emergent-badge') in the bottom-right corner. At 390px viewport, this badge occupies roughly the right 165px of the bottom row, the same vertical band as <nav data-testid='mobile-nav'>. Because the badge is rendered after the nav and has a higher z-index, document.elementFromPoint at the center of the 4th, 5th, and 6th nav items returns the badge anchor. Mitigation: in /app/frontend/src/components/MobileNav.jsx, replace `flex justify-between` with `flex justify-start gap-1` and add `pr-32` (or `max-w-[calc(100%-160px)]`) to keep all items within the visible left region; alternatively reduce mobile items to 5 by moving one (e.g., Tips) into a Studio sub-tab. Reproduction: open https://resina-palette-craft.preview.emergentagent.com/ at viewport 390x844, tap 'A/B' in bottom nav — URL does not change.
